candidates.title: Multi-Child Proportional Ablation + Causal Validation

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Defines absorption phenomenon; uses single-feature ablation metric. Foundation for the proposal's core concept.

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2026. Sanity Checks for SAEs. arXiv:2602.14111
collisions.overlap: Uses identical baseline methodology (random decoder, shuffled features, permuted encoder) on SAEs. On synthetic ground-truth, SAEs recover only 9% of true features despite 71% explained variance. Proposes Korznikov-style baselines.

collisions.paper: Tang et al., 2025. On the Theoretical Foundation of Sparse Dictionary Learning in Mechanistic Interpretability. arXiv:2512.05534
collisions.overlap: Proved spurious local minima correspond to feature absorption. Provides theoretical grounding for why absorption occurs. CRITICAL NEW CITATION NEEDED.

collisions.paper: O'Brien et al., 2024. Steering Language Model Refusal with SAEs. arXiv:2411.11296
collisions.overlap: Shows SAE steering improves safety but causes capability degradation on other tasks. Related to H3 (causal steering) but does not test absorbed vs. non-absorbed features specifically.

collisions.paper: Borobia et al., 2026. How Pruning Reshapes Features. arXiv:2603.25325
collisions.overlap: Found rare features survive pruning better (Spearman rho = -1.0). Related to H2 (frequency-absorption correlation) but in different context (weight pruning vs. SAE training).

candidates.differentiation_notes: Multi-child proportional ablation (k=5) is a specific extension of Chanin et al.'s single-feature ablation, designed to fix pilot saturation. The variance-of-contributions formulation is novel. Causal steering validation (H3) tests absorbed vs. non-absorbed features specifically, which O'Brien et al. do not address. Safety-critical absorption (H_Safe) is genuinely unexplored.
critical_citations_needed: Tang et al. (arXiv:2512.05534) -- MUST cite for theoretical grounding
Borobia et al. (arXiv:2603.25325) -- cite for H2 related evidence
risks: Tang et al. provides theoretical backing that must be acknowledged and built upon
Korznikov (2026) preempts baseline methodology but not absorption-specific metrics or causal steering
Borobia et al.'s frequency correlation is in different context but suggests similar effects

candidates.title: Competitive Exclusion Framing

collisions.paper: Kalmykov & Kalmykov, 2012. A Refinement of the Competitive Exclusion Principle. arXiv:1211.1869
collisions.overlap: Foundational ecology reference for competitive exclusion principle.

collisions.paper: Blumenthal & Mehta, 2023. Geometry of Ecological Coexistence. arXiv:2304.10694
collisions.overlap: Geometry of ecological coexistence. Related to niche geometry framing.

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Discusses sparsity optimization as cause of absorption but does not frame as competitive exclusion dynamics.

collisions.paper: Tang et al., 2025. On the Theoretical Foundation of SDL in MI. arXiv:2512.05534
collisions.overlap: Proves spurious local minima = absorption. Theoretical complement to ecological framing.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No prior work in ML/CS applies competitive exclusion to SAE interpretability. arXiv search returned NO matches for 'competitive exclusion sparse autoencoder'. Tang et al. provides theoretical complement. Ecological framing is novel but must be validated empirically (H_Comp: absorption increases monotonically with hierarchy strength).
critical_citations_needed: Tang et al. (arXiv:2512.05534) -- theoretical complement to ecological framing
risks: Ecological parallels are motivating theory, not established fact for neural networks
Must validate H_Comp empirically (monotonic absorption-overlap relationship)

candidates.title: Safety-Critical Features Are Disproportionately Absorbed

collisions.paper: Bhargav & Zhu, 2025. Feature-Guided SAE Steering for Refusal-Rate Control. arXiv:2511.00029
collisions.overlap: Uses SAE steering for safety. Does NOT address whether safety features are disproportionately absorbed.

collisions.paper: O'Brien et al., 2024. Steering Language Model Refusal with SAEs. arXiv:2411.11296
collisions.overlap: SAE steering for safety. Does NOT address absorption in safety features.

collisions.paper: GSAE, 2025. Graph-Regularized SAE for Robust LLM Safety Steering. arXiv:2512.06655
collisions.overlap: Safety steering with distributed features. Does NOT address absorption.

collisions.paper: SAILS, 2025. Interpretable Safety Alignment via SAE-Constructed Subspace. arXiv:2512.23260
collisions.overlap: Safety subspace from SAE decoder directions. Does NOT address absorption.

collisions.paper: Goldowsky-Dill et al., 2025. Detecting Strategic Deception Using Linear Probes. arXiv:2502.03407
collisions.overlap: Probing deception features in LLMs. Does NOT involve SAEs or absorption.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No prior work examines whether safety-critical features are systematically MORE absorbed by SAEs. Extensive search for 'safety-critical features absorbed sparse autoencoder' returned NO matches. The contrarian framing (SAE-based safety analysis unreliable precisely for cases that matter most) is genuinely novel and compelling.
critical_citations_needed: Bhargav & Zhu (arXiv:2511.00029) -- SAE steering for safety
risks: Requires human annotation of safety-critical features
Small sample size (20 safety + 20 control) may limit statistical power

candidates.title: Niche Geometry Diagnostic

collisions.paper: Tang et al., 2025. On the Theoretical Foundation of SDL in MI. arXiv:2512.05534
collisions.overlap: Proved spurious local minima correspond to feature absorption. Local minima have geometric interpretation. containment_ratio operationalizes this theoretical insight. CRITICAL NEW CITATION.

collisions.paper: Korznikov et al., 2025. OrtSAE. arXiv:2509.22033
collisions.overlap: Uses encoder-decoder geometry for MITIGATION (orthogonal constraints). Proposal uses geometry for DETECTION. Different goals.

collisions.paper: Blumenthal & Mehta, 2023. Geometry of Ecological Coexistence. arXiv:2304.10694
collisions.overlap: Ecological geometry framing. Related to niche geometry concept.

candidates.differentiation_notes: OrtSAE uses geometry for MITIGATION; cand_geom uses the same geometry for DETECTION. These are different goals. Tang et al. provides the theoretical connection between local minima and absorption that motivates geometry-based prediction. No prior work claims containment_ratio predicts absorption.
critical_citations_needed: Tang et al. (arXiv:2512.05534) -- MUST cite for theoretical grounding of geometry-local minima connection
risks: Tang et al.'s theoretical result means geometry-based prediction is well-motivated; must cite explicitly
Must validate AUC > 0.75 on held-out features

candidates.title: Multi-Resolution SAE Ensemble

collisions.paper: Gadgil et al., 2025. Ensembling Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2505.16077
collisions.overlap: Ensembles SAEs with different random INITIALIZATIONS. Proposal uses different L0 TARGETS. Different ensemble strategy and goal.

collisions.paper: Muchane et al., 2025. Incorporating Hierarchical Semantics in SAE. arXiv:2506.01197
collisions.overlap: Models hierarchical feature structure in SAE architecture. Proposal detects hierarchy POST-HOC via L0 variation. Different approach.

collisions.paper: Luo et al., 2026. From Atoms to Trees. arXiv:2602.11881
collisions.overlap: Hierarchical feature forest with parent-child relationships. Models hierarchy in architecture. Proposal is complementary.

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2025. Sparse but Wrong. arXiv:2508.16560
collisions.overlap: Shows L0 affects feature correctness. Related evidence for L0 importance.

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2025. Feature Hedging. arXiv:2505.11756
collisions.overlap: Correlated features break narrow SAEs. Related to feature interaction.

candidates.differentiation_notes: L0-variation ensemble (L0=16, 64, 256) is mechanistically distinct from Gadgil's initialization-variation ensemble. L0 diversity as mechanism for hierarchical recovery is not claimed in prior work. Must clearly distinguish from Gadgil (initialization) and Muchane/Luo (architectural hierarchy). Consider emphasizing 'L0-variation' rather than generic 'ensemble'.
critical_citations_needed: Gadgil et al. (arXiv:2505.16077) -- must differentiate from initialization diversity
Muchane et al. (arXiv:2506.01197) -- complementary (architecture vs. post-hoc)
Luo et al. (arXiv:2602.11881) -- complementary (architecture vs. post-hoc)
risks: Gadgil already showed ensemble diversity captures more features
Muchane and Luo on hierarchical SAE published recently (Jun 2025, Feb 2026)
Claim that high-L0 SAE captures absorbed child features needs experimental validation

overall_assessment: All candidates score 5+ (none require dropping). cand_safe is most novel (9/10, no prior work on safety-critical absorption). cand_p1 (8/10) and cand_eco (8/10) are clearly novel on measurement methodology and ecological framing. cand_geom (7/10) is novel on detection vs. mitigation distinction. cand_ens (5/10) overlaps with Gadgil but is differentiated by L0 diversity mechanism. CRITICAL: Tang et al. (arXiv:2512.05534) provides theoretical grounding for multiple hypotheses and must be cited. ADDITIONAL: Basu et al. (arXiv:2603.18353) provides critical validation that steering interventions often fail, supporting honest negative result reporting for H3.
critical_findings: Tang et al. (arXiv:2512.05534) -- proved spurious local minima correspond to feature absorption. CRITICAL citation for cand_p1, cand_geom, cand_eco
Basu et al. (arXiv:2603.18353) -- SAE steering produced zero effect despite 3,695 significant features. Supports H3 as potential negative result.
Luo et al. (arXiv:2602.11881) -- Hierarchical SAE (Feb 2026) is complementary to cand_ens
Borobia et al. (arXiv:2603.25325) -- rare features survive pruning better (rho=-1.0), related to H2
Bhargav & Zhu (arXiv:2511.00029), GSAE (2512.06655), SAILS (2512.23260) -- safety steering work that does NOT address absorption
No prior work examines whether safety-critical features are disproportionately absorbed
No prior work applies competitive exclusion to SAE interpretability

recommendation_summary.cand_p1: proceed -- measurement fix + safety-critical analysis clearly differentiated
recommendation_summary.cand_eco: proceed -- novel ecological framing, validate empirically
recommendation_summary.cand_geom: proceed -- detection vs. OrtSAE mitigation clearly differentiated, cite Tang et al.
recommendation_summary.cand_ens: proceed_with_repositioning -- emphasize L0-variation mechanism, distinguish from Gadgil and Muchane/Luo

required_citations.reason: CRITICAL: theoretical grounding for absorption local minima, relevant to H1, cand_geom, cand_eco
CRITICAL: shows SAE steering has zero effect despite significant features; validates H3 as potential negative result
H2 related evidence: frequency effects on feature survival
